message:
feat(pager): WAL append gate to serialize same-process concurrent writer commits

Concurrent writers sharing the same process were bouncing off the
cross-process EXCLUSIVE file-lock escalation path during WAL commit,
surfacing avoidable Busy retries. This adds a per-database mutex gate
that queues in-process WAL commits through a narrow critical section,
eliminating lock contention between same-process concurrent writers
while preserving cross-process exclusion via the existing file locks.

- Add WalAppendGate type alias and global WAL_APPEND_GATES registry
  keyed by database path
- Wire wal_append_gate into SimpleTransaction so commit acquires
  the in-process mutex before escalating the file lock
- Gate is only acquired in WAL journal mode; rollback journal mode
  is unaffected
- Fix bench query_row() temporary lifetime in mixed DML benchmark

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
